name: Portland Gold Buyers, LLC
description_short: Portland Gold Buyers, LLC is a local precious metals buyer in downtown Portland offering cash payments for gold, silver, platinum jewelry, coins, and bullion with in-person appraisals.
description_long:
Portland Gold Buyers, LLC operates as a precious metals and jewelry buyer located in the Willamette Building in downtown Portland (534 SW 3rd Ave, Suite 305). The company specializes in purchasing unwanted gold, silver, and platinum items for immediate cash payment. Founded on the principle of providing fair, transparent transactions, the business emphasizes local, face-to-face dealings rather than mail-in or remote sales.

The company's core services include purchasing gold jewelry (10K through 24K), silver bullion and coins, platinum items, dental gold, raw gold (nuggets, flakes, dust), and various bullion forms including bars, coins from multiple countries (American Eagles, Krugerrands, Maple Leafs, etc.), and specialty items. They also offer diamond and jewelry appraisals and provide pawn loans against jewelry collateral. The website includes extensive educational content on precious metals valuation, gold testing methods, and market information.

Portland Gold Buyers emphasizes competitive pricing, claiming they "pay more" than competitors and encouraging customers to "shop around first" before returning to them. They offer flexible hours (Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday by appointment call), accept walk-ins but prefer appointments, and conduct all testing, weighing, and appraisals on-site in front of customers. The company operates with emphasis on discretion, local service, and immediate payment with no minimums or waiting periods.

As a precious metals buyer rather than a traditional pawn shop offering loans, Portland Gold Buyers is primarily a cash buyer for unwanted items rather than a lender. While they mention offering pawn loans, their website focus is predominantly on buying precious metals outright. Customers should note that actual prices paid will depend on current market rates and the purity of items, and the competitive pricing claims are unverified.
pros: On-site testing, weighing, and appraisal conducted in front of customers for transparency
Flexible hours including Saturday availability by appointment (more than standard business hours)
No minimums or waiting periods—immediate cash payment at time of transaction
Accepts diverse precious metals forms: jewelry, coins, bullion bars, dental gold, raw gold, and specialty international coins
Local, in-person face-to-face transactions in downtown Portland location
Extensive educational content on precious metals, valuation methods, and market information
Discretion and privacy emphasized as part of service philosophy
cons: Pricing claims of 'paying more' are unverified and not substantiated with market comparisons or rate sheets
Limited geographic service area—downtown Portland location only, no mail-in or remote buying option mentioned
Website lacks customer reviews, testimonials, ratings, or third-party verification of service quality or fair pricing
No transparent pricing guide or current offer rates published online; customers must contact for quotes
Appointment preference may create inconvenience despite walk-in acceptance
best_for: Individuals with unwanted gold, silver, or platinum jewelry seeking immediate local cash sales
Collectors or investors liquidating precious metals coins and bullion holdings
People needing quick cash who own precious metals and prefer in-person transactions with transparent on-site testing
diagnosis: Portland Gold Buyers is best for Portland-area residents with precious metals or jewelry they wish to sell for immediate cash, who value transparent in-person transactions and on-site testing. The main caveat is that pricing claims are unverified online, and customers should obtain competitive quotes and confirm current market rates before selling, as actual prices depend on spot market values and item purity.
services: Gold and silver coins (American Eagles, Krugerrands, Maple Leafs, etc.)
On-site precious metals testing and weighing
International gold coins and specialty bullion forms
